content: Just as the double meaning of reflexion (both linguistic reflection in language learning and physical light reflection) relies on aligning a bounced signal/utterance back to the source to refine understanding, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the pedagogical framework of high school language writing reflection to improve self-correction. This frames LLM self-reflection not just as a computational consistency check, but as a guided iterative writing-style revision practice where the LLM acts as its own language learner responding to instructor-style feedback pulled from its multiple solution outputs.
